industry: Motion Control, Power Transmission and Automation / Specialty Industrial Machinery
thesis: Regal Rexnord is a diversified industrial maker of electric motors, power transmission, and automation/motion-control products that has quietly become an AI data-center beneficiary. Its Automation & Motion Control (AMC) segment is booming on data-center power management, precision motion, and cooling demand — Q4 2025 brought ~$735M of data-center orders and Q1 2026 AMC orders grew over 34%. FY2025 revenue was roughly flat at $5.93B, but adjusted EPS reached $9.65 with $893M of adjusted free cash flow driving rapid deleveraging, and management guides FY2026 to $10.20-$11.00 adjusted EPS (~10% growth). Unlike most AI-adjacent names, RRX is not expensive: at ~$207 it trades at only ~18-19x forward earnings with a Strong Buy consensus and a ~$260 target (~26% upside). Reasonable valuation, a real data-center catalyst, margin expansion (gross margin up from 26% to ~38% over seven years), and deleveraging optionality make this a BUY, with elevated leverage and industrial cyclicality the key risks.

drivers: Data-center orders (AMC +34% YoY, ~$735M booked in Q4 2025) convert into accelerating organic revenue growth through 2026-2027
Adjusted EBITDA margin expands beyond the 22.2% FY2026 guide as higher-margin automation and data-center mix scales
Deleveraging from ~3.1x toward ~2.7x net debt/EBITDA completes, freeing capital for buybacks and bolt-on M&A
Cross-selling integrated powertrain and automation solutions lifts share and pricing across aerospace/defense, discrete automation, and medical
New CEO Aamir Paul sustains the margin and growth playbook and the stock re-rates toward peer automation multiples

base_case.assumptions: Regal Rexnord delivers FY2026 adjusted EPS of $10.20-$11.00 (~10% growth) on ~4.5% sales growth and a 22.2% adjusted EBITDA margin, with data-center orders steadily converting to revenue and tariffs mitigated to dollar-cost neutral by mid-2026. Free cash flow of ~$650M funds continued deleveraging. The stock reaches the ~$260 analyst consensus over the next year as growth and margins are delivered.

risks: Data-center and automation order momentum stalls or converts to revenue more slowly than expected, leaving organic growth soft
Tariffs, input costs, and unfavorable mix pressure the adjusted EBITDA margin below guidance
Elevated leverage (~3.1x net debt/EBITDA) constrains flexibility if the industrial cycle turns down
A broad industrials de-rating hits a stock that has already risen ~50% in 2026
CEO transition and integration/execution missteps disrupt the margin-expansion trajectory

catalysts.event: Q2 2026 earnings — orders/backlog, data-center revenue conversion, and margin/tariff progress
Conversion of ~$735M data-center orders and 34%+ AMC order growth into reported revenue
Execution against FY2026 guidance: adjusted EPS $10.20-$11.00, ~4.5% sales growth, 22.2% adjusted EBITDA margin
Deleveraging toward ~2.7x net debt/EBITDA, unlocking buybacks/M&A; tariff mitigation to dollar-cost neutral by mid-2026
CEO transition to Aamir Paul and continued gross-margin expansion (26% to ~38% over seven years)

key_risks: Elevated leverage: net debt/adjusted EBITDA near 3.1x, which amplifies downside if the industrial cycle weakens
Cyclicality: motors, power transmission, and industrial automation demand track manufacturing and capex cycles
Execution/conversion risk: strong data-center orders must convert to revenue and margin as guided
Tariff, input-cost, and mix headwinds that could pressure margins despite mitigation plans
The stock has risen ~50% in 2026, so a portion of the data-center optimism is already priced in
CEO transition (Louis Pinkham to Aamir Paul) and integration execution risk
